chunk_summary: The core problem in EdTech for 2026 is not ad copy or funnels — it's a trust deficit. Lead costs are rising (CPL went from ₹100 to ₹500-600 in 4-5 years, CAC at ₹20-25K for a ₹50-60K product) because platforms are saturated, data privacy restricts tracking, and customers need 8-10 touchpoints before buying. People buy because they trust you and your consistent, authentic presence.
content: If you are an edtech founder, then I'm sure you can relate with this. Your lead costs are rising. Your marketing aspect is getting heavier each month. And when it comes to closing, it has become more harder because the leads are not trusting you anymore. And no matter how much you optimize, scale or tweak your ad ecosystem, something remains fundamentally broken. Well, the problem is not about your ads or the ad copy or the funnels. The problem is the market that we live in and lack the trust. ... people do not buy because they like your ad or how good your offer looks. They buy because they trust you and your presence is consistent and authentic. Right now after interacting with so many tech founders, what I've seen is everybody's chasing the next shiny object. Whether it's that next AI funnel, that's next magical script or another agent that can come in and automate your business. But I'm sure you know the truth if you have been in the game long enough that the real problem is the trust deficit. People don't trust you enough before they buy from you. And this is why 2026 belongs to those who solve the trust problem, not the tactical problem. ... costs are rising for three main reasons number one platform saturation so many tech companies are competing fiercely on major sites like Meta and Google which drives up the prices. Secondly, data privacy restrictions. New rules about privacy mean ad platform cannot track people which leads to inefficient budget allocation especially iPhone users I'm talking about here and what they are doing with meta and the trust deficit customer do not trust the ads right away they need to check and confirm everything before they buy. As I mentioned to you in today's time, people need at least 8 to 10 touch points before they make a decision. ... on an average in today's time at least people need 8 to 10 touch points maybe more in today's time because and this is going to increase because there's so much noise that going to come in coming years only the company which stand for authenticity which is present everywhere will win the game.

What is the trust deficit in EdTech marketing?
How many touchpoints does a customer need before buying?
Why is authenticity more important than ad optimization?

chunk_summary: The solution is an omnichannel trust system: stop relying on one channel (spray and pray) and build 'intent-based visibility' where your brand appears everywhere a customer checks. The buying journey is now a validation loop — conversions happen because your presence across platforms confirms your claims. Proof equals presence. Be consistent, authentic, and show real results.
content: So the right way to fix this step is to stop the spray and prey method and build a strong high trust omni channel system. now which is basically based on high intent and visibility. So omnichannel marketing means intent based visibility for attack. This is where your brand is visible by showing up everywhere a potential customer looks to check on. Secondly by giving them the exact information they need to feel confident at that moment. All right the high trust buying story. Okay the validation loop the part to purchase is now continuous loop where the customer checks your claim on different platforms. Number one conversions happen but not because of one good ad. Core principle in 2026 is proof is equal to your presence. the more you are present and talking about your authentic self, authentic branding, authentic results that you producing for your students or for your cohorts. That's what exactly that matters, right? ... So what's the solution? Now this document will give you the five-step omni channel framework which we use personally in our business and of course we share with our EdTech founders who work with us as well in our scale community.

How does the validation loop work in buying decisions?

chunk_summary: Step 1 — High-intent audience segmentation: shift paid ads from broad targeting to only people showing high interest. Use ads for micro-conversions (email signups, not final sale). Target based on behavioral signals (e.g., viewed pricing but didn't buy). Meta/Instagram are interest-based; Google/YouTube are intent-based with higher-cost but higher-quality leads.
content: So first high intent audience segmentation. Of course, these are some tactical changes that you can make on the ad front. Okay. So, change your paid advertising from targeting huge groups to targeting only the people who show high interest, which is called high intent. And that's where Meta and Google are different. Meta and Instagram are more towards interest based ad ecosystem. Google and YouTube are more intent based ad ecosystem. That's why the cost on Google if you are running the ads on Google platform are slightly higher because it gives more high intent buyers as compared to meta. So use ads only to get micro conversions like small email sign up not the final sale. Target customers based on specific behavioral signals like people who look at the price but do not buy. So all these are some smart tactic. Again we can go very deep into that as well. If you are having a marketing person they know how to do it.

chunk_summary: Step 2 — Email nurturing automation: most companies, even those doing multiple crores per month, lack proper email nurturing. Build a 90-day conveyor belt of educational, value-add emails (not reminders). Mix educational tips, success stories, case studies, and testimonials. Set up lead scoring based on opens and clicks. Only send high-scoring leads to the sales team to save time and money.
content: the most important aspect which I would highly recommend you to add in your ecosystem. Now I connect with the companies doing multiple CR a month. They also do not have this and I'm very very honest about it. They do not have a ecosystem of nurturing the leads through emails. Okay. Specifically if your audience is working professional parents on B2B as well like principles HOD's placement ads they do read the emails okay or even in fact if you're targeting corporates they do read their emails as well okay so use email to run your lead nurturing automation now again I'm not talking about reminder kind of emails I'm talking about real nurturing where you really add the value ... Set up a strong lead scoring system based on what people do, opening the email, clicking the links. Use nurturing automation map to send weekly valuable content. Again, we have a 90-day conveyor belt email nurturing ecosystem that we have built inside our skilled community which again we provide to our skilled members in which basically we share these three things that you need to do. Your email should be more educational, something they are not aware of, some strategies, some tips, some success stories and of course case studies, testimonials you need to share what change you are bringing in the life of your students or your cohorts that are joining you. All right. And only send leads with a high enough score to your sales team which saves the time and money. That's how you can efficiently utilize the resource of your sales team as well.

chunk_summary: Step 3 — YouTube for long-form authority: YouTube is essential for B2C and B2B EdTech. Short-form (Reels, Shorts) gets volume; long-form builds trust. Make videos answering exact customer questions and objections your sales team hears daily. This establishes expertise, serves as an SEO bridge (ranks on Google and YouTube), and directly addresses the trust deficit. Long-form content creates buyers; short-form gets eyeballs.
content: Third is YouTube. Now YouTube going to is the one platform which I will highly recommend you need to be there whether you are into B2C B2B if you are running a tech company today you need to be there on YouTube okay YouTube is one platform where people can check your long- form content short form content like real shorts and all they just create the engagement they do not create the buyers specifically if you are into mid to high ticket sales specifically if your product goes from 30 to 40,000 goes up to 5 lakh 10 lakh or more than that as well. YouTube is one platform where people consume your long- form content and if people are watching your long form content the more they will relate with your brand the more time they are spending to listen to what you have to share and the more trust you will be able to generate so that's why if you are into the tech domain irrespective B2B B2C B2G whatever that you are make sure you are present on YouTube and create long- form content right so build long-term authority by creating group of content focused on customer questions specifically what questions your customers are asking your students are asking you if you don't know ask your sales team to give you plethora of questions they can give you what objections they get what questions they get in the sales call create the content on that right that's how you can prevent the objections and same content you can use in the email as well to nurture your leads answer those questions which your customers are asking which your leads are asking to your sales team. Answer those questions in your content. That's the best strategy. Right? So, make videos that answer the exact problems your students are searching for. That's what exactly I shared with you. This makes you as an expert and act as a strong SEO bridge. High people find you on Google and YouTube. As I mentioned to you, when you going to people going to search about you on Google, your videos will be ranked at the top and that's where they will again come to you and see you as an expert. And again it will help you to bridge that trust deficit that is there existing in today's market.

chunk_summary: Step 4 — Short-form micro-expertise demonstrations: use Reels, Shorts, and TikTok for quick expertise showcases. Focus on one quick useful lesson per video, not too much content. Topics: mistakes to avoid, strategies, mindset, roadmaps. The goal is attention and virality, not direct sales — drive viewers to free lead magnets, newsletters, or webinars. Short-form = volume; long-form = trust.
content: Short form microexpert demonstration. Now these are the now this content is basically for to get more eyeballs more visibility. Long form content will create trust in your in the mind of the users. Short form content will get you more volume but not the trust. That's where your volume will gain will also going to improve. That's why long form content is very very important but at the same time to increase your reach, increase your presence, increase more eyeballs, you need to create short form content as well. Right? So the rule so use short videos, real shorts, Tik Tok in case it works in your geography for quickly showing your expertise. These are microexperties demonstration. Again, educate your audience. Talk about the mistakes they're supposed to avoid. For example, if you are helping them to crack any exam like skate, GMAT, SAT, talk about the strategies. Talk about the mindset. Talk about the study action plan road map the people the students can follow, the mistakes they can avoid. If you are doing any kind of IT related programs like details, Oracle training, IT training or agentic AI for professionals, talk about the tools, talk about how agentic is replacing people, right? So all those things you can talk about, right? The entire focus is on getting the attention basically getting the eyeballs or you can say chasing virality in different words right so this is the key mantra on short form content okay let me highlight this as well so keep your video focus on one quick useful lesson not too much of content because 60 second 90 second we have or 30 second this let prospect check you out easily and conversions happen because your knowledge is confirmed over time or at least it will start building the conversation. Take them towards your free lead magnet. Take them towards your signups whether it's a free webinar, free call booking or free any kind of PDF checklist whatever that you have created and that's why I would highly recommend have multiple lead magnets in today's time whether it's PDF whether it's blogs whether it's videos whether it's some kind of documents have multiple free lead magnets that you can take let them in. That's the whole goal of short form content. Let them subscribe to your email newsletters, emails as well and then you nurture them over a period of time.

chunk_summary: Step 5 — Retargeting ads for mid/bottom funnel: run objection-based campaigns for people who entered your funnel but didn't buy. Address their specific worries (price, trust, lack of testimonials). Use consequence framing — show what they lose by not upgrading. Create lookalike audiences from high-intent leads. This shortens sales cycles by handling doubts before the sales call.
content: Last is fifth point retargeting ads of course these are objection based campaigns basically. So finish the process with using specific ad campaign to handle the routes. Now here we are talking about mid and bottom of the funnel people. Now if you know marketing I'm sure you must be aware top of the funnel which we call tofu. Middle of the funnel mofu bottom of the funnel bofu. So this strategy going to work for middle of the funnel and bottom of the funnel. All others I shared is for top of the funnel. Yes, email is also used as a mid and bottom because you are nurturing the lead. But this is important to retarget the people who have been there in your ecosystem but not buying from you. You can create a lookalike as well whether it's Google platform or meta platform. You can create the lookalike of the high intent lead and target pan India. If you are selling an international market you can target that country as well. whether it's Middle East or US or UK. So, the entire strategy basically is show different retargeting ads based on where the customer left your website. As I mentioned to you, these are middle and bottom of the funnel. These are the people basically who have who are there in your funnel who have joined the funnel, join your free lead magnets, took the sales call also maybe but have not purchased because of certain reasons. Your price is too high, they don't trust you, they did not like your testimonials, they do not have any testimonials and all. So, you need to do the retargeting ads like that. Okay. If they do not learn AI agents, how to bring that in their day-to-day work, right? Talk about the investment and what they will miss out on. Consequences analysis. Okay, that's what we call play around the consequences. what they will lose out on and what change they will feel or what change will come in their career if they upgrade let's say if they're doing a 10 LPA job if they can move to 15 LPA job or 16 18 LPA job look at the difference by joining your 60,000 agent program that's a huge shift right this is how you need to understand from the psychology of the buyer when you are running the retargeting ads

chunk_summary: The 5-step flywheel summary: combine all channels so a prospect sees you everywhere — YouTube long-form, short-form, email nurturing, retargeting ads, and newsletters — creating 8+ touchpoints. This omnichannel presence builds trust over time. Implement for at least 90 days to see measurable improvement in conversion rates, shorter sales cycles, and lower customer acquisition costs.
content: So this is the final step where we are focusing on 7 to eight touch points. So we are generating content on YouTube long format short format email is running then we have our retargeting ads as well on whether it's meta platform you're using or Google then you are having your newsletters as well. So this is how basically you are using omni channel presence and this is the five-step process or five-step flywheel method that I will share with you to implement in your EdTech ecosystem. If you're not using, if you really want to win in 2026, otherwise you will keep on seeing the cost getting higher and higher and you are still stuck at the same level where you were stuck from last one year or two year in your business because I'm working with these kind of tech founders.
